Beer Stained Coldain Tunic  
 

Lore Item
Slot: CHEST
AC: 12
STR: +5 STA: +5 HP: +15
SV COLD: +5 SV MAGIC: +5 SV POISON: +5
WT: 2.0 Size: MEDIUM
Class: WAR CLR PAL RNG SHD DRU ROG BER
Race: DWF HFL GNM FRG
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)

This item lists Beastlords as being able to wear it, but I've never seen a Dwarven, Gnomish, or Halfling Beastlord. I don't even think they are possible Beastlord optional races. Just an observation. Thanks for listening all.

This is an old pic .. as of 7-7-03 the Tunic I have is listed as changing the Race to take off MNK, BRD, & SHM ... they also ADDED the FRG to the Class ... sending a pic to Allak as we speak, will see how long it takes to update :o)

So, to surmize the resists and such will read the same, but the Class will read WAR CLR PAL RNG SHD DRU ROG & Race will read DWF HLF GNM FRG

Hope this helps

▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Aug 29 2001 at 7:05 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Your smokeing crack, the BCP SUCKS TOTALY for the end game, now granted, the druids role in raids is very limited, but I would take the resists on the thing anyday over the +9 dex( you got anything that random procs??)
RE: EH...
QuoteReply
# Sep 30 2001 at 5:52 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592251300172
Scholar
20 posts
Well, given that DEX controls disrupts and fizzles, I'd say it's a useful stat to have.
Like, say, when a mob breaks on you and the lead calls evac. Sure the evacer is supposed to sit there and never cast, but things can happen.
Or when you're a druid solo kiting and you root to med for mana to finish a mob, and the root fizzles. Or breaks and you're trying to self gate away before being turned into a fine red paste.
Lastly, yes you shouldn't be meleeing, but the time comes when you might have to. Once you get the WIS to 200, you have to think about the other stats.
Not that the BCP is an end game item, of course, but you get the point.
▲ Page top
RE: EH...
QuoteReply
# Oct 15 2001 at 3:44 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592248526746
29 posts
First of all, DEX does not control disrupts and fizzles.

Secondly, disrupts and fizzles are two completely different things (although the result in-game is very much the same =).

Your fizzle rate depends ONLY on your skill level in the school of the spell which you try to cast. So casting root with a skill level of 5 in alteration will leave you with a very high fizzle rate.

Disrupts depends ONLY on your channelling skill level. The higher your channelling skill, the higher the chance of regaining your concentrating and continue casting.

Not flaming, just trying to clear out a common misunderstanding. =)
▲ Page top
RE: EH...
QuoteReply
# Jul 22 2001 at 2:39 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593361532627
View User Forum
54 posts
That is only your opinion, as the BCP does not have any resistances, or any HP bonus. It's all really want you want on it, considering they are both the same AC. Besides, the BCP weighs 10.0, this is only 2.0

▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Mar 23 2001 at 11:16 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) IF IT SAYS DWARF, GNOME, HALF ELF WHY DOES IT SAY MONK? (ISKAR/HUMAN) ERR.... HHMMMMMM COME ON VERANT, THIS WOULD MAKE A GOOD MONK TUNIC FOR LOWER LEVELS.
RE: Why constantly state the obvious?
QuoteReply
# Mar 25 2001 at 5:28 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
The reason it says monk is because this is wearable by all leather wearing classes. Its alot easier for verant to filter out armor by class instead of by race. This armor is actually only wearable by the WAR, CLR, PAL, DRU, & ROG classes. Those are the only classes that can be the 3 small races listed. Small leather armor also lists the same classes as this even though almost half of those classes can't be a small race.

Now I wish people would quit always stating the obvious and complaining when a class is listed on a piece of armor even though they can't be the races listed.
▲ Page top

RE: Wearable...
QuoteReply
# Dec 21 2000 at 7:58 PM Rating: Decent
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
For the love of god shut up. Havent yall noticed this before? When they label what classes can wear the item, they don't look at race. They put it through a filter so it will automatically be labeled for all who can wear leather. For the race, they just filtered out shorties.

Panoyen
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Dec 27 2000 at 7:13 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) If sm race only why not DE,
RE: Wearable...
QuoteReply
# May 15 2001 at 6:14 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Because there's small races, and then there's SHORTIES.

This is shorty only:)
▲ Page top
Nonsense
QuoteReply
# Dec 22 2000 at 8:44 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
I understand that, but why would they include a class, and exclude the only races that can play that class? There is no such thing as a dwarf, halfling, or gnome monk, ranger, shadow knight, bard, or shaman, so why would they list those classes? I understand that there are no gnome paladins, but paladin is included by virtue of the dwarf paladin being a viable choice.

/boggle
▲ Page top
RE: Nonsense
QuoteReply
# Dec 26 2000 at 8:22 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Because they put the filter for leather wearers on it as well.

Aka. They filtered for short people
aka. They filtered for leather type armor classes

End of story. People read far too much into things like this. Lighten up
